description_long: This is a large, hand-crafted ceramic vase with a wide, slightly flared rim and a rounded, tapering body. The surface displays a weathered, matte finish with visible patches of discoloration and wear, suggesting age and exposure. Around the upper midsection, a raised, twisted band encircles the vase, providing both visual and tactile contrast. Above this band, faint incised wavy lines and geometric motifs are etched into the surface, adding subtle decorative detail. The coloration is predominantly earthy, with shades of brown, gray, and muted orange blending irregularly across the surface. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with traditional pottery techniques.
description: A large, weathered ceramic vase with a flared rim, raised twisted band, and faint incised wavy patterns in earthy brown and gray tones.
description_view_0: This view highlights the flared rim and the twisted band, with visible incised wavy lines and a mix of brown and gray coloration.
description_view_1: The front shows more of the orange-brown surface and the decorative band, with the incised patterns less pronounced but still visible.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the contrast between the dark gray and lighter brown areas, with the wavy incised motif running clearly above the band.
description_view_3: The rear view displays the most continuous stretch of the incised wavy line and a smoother gradient of earthy colors, with less visible surface damage.
